title: Tris Buffered Saline (TBS)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ca89r","text":"A buffer solution has the function of resisting changes in pH even when adding powerful acids or bases. However, in the physiological environment the buffered system also provides cofactors for enzymatic reactions, critical salts and even essential nutrients for cells and tissues. Therefore, when trying to reproduce biological conditions in vitro, we must make the appropriate choice of the buffer. After all, it will provide the appropriate medium in which reactions will occur.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9dvj6","text":"Organize your workspace.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3viof","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"852b3","text":"Make sure all solutions and equipment are available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"753nr","text":"Wear personal protective equipment: gloves, lab coat and mask.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: pH 7.4 10 mM Tris 150 mM NaCl Prepare 1 L, of TBS by dissolving 1.21 g, Tris Base and 8.7 g, NaCl in 1 L, of distilled water .
Adjust pH before use. Tris has a pKa of 8.3. Hence, the buffering capacity at pH 7.4 is minimal compared to phosphate buffer (pKa = 7.21)
creator: Neilier Junior
